name: Inconsistent naming conventions within a file
description: When a file contains a mixture of naming conventions (snake_case and camelCase), it violates consistency principles and increases cognitive load. All identifiers within a file should follow the same naming convention.

detection_details.collect_identifiers: Extract all function names, variable names, class names, method names from the AST
detection_details.classify_pattern: For each identifier, classify as snake_case, camelCase, PascalCase, or other
detection_details.detect_mixing: If file contains both snake_case and camelCase identifiers of the same type (e.g., functions), flag as inconsistent
detection_details.threshold: At least 3 identifiers of each convention needed to confirm mixing is intentional, not accidental

per_language_rules.python: Should use snake_case only; camelCase is violation
per_language_rules.javascript: Should use camelCase for variables/functions; snake_case is violation
per_language_rules.typescript: Should use camelCase for variables/functions, PascalCase for classes/interfaces

exclusions: External/library API calls (cannot control their naming)
Test files with different conventions for test methods
Config files imported from external sources
false_positive_conditions: Files that import both snake_case Python modules and camelCase JS libraries
Migration code in progress with intentional mixed conventions
remediation: Choose the appropriate naming convention for the language and refactor all identifiers to follow it consistently. Use automated refactoring tools to rename across the file.
fix_playbook:
1. Identify which naming convention is appropriate for the language
2. List all identifiers that don't follow the convention
3. Use IDE refactoring to rename identifiers consistently
4. Ensure snake_case Python code doesn't mix with camelCase
5. For JavaScript/TypeScript: ensure functions/variables use camelCase
6. Update any related documentation

SARIF_mapping.name: Inconsistent naming conventions within a file

agent_semantics.summary: Mixed naming conventions detected in file - all identifiers should follow consistent style
review_questions: Is this file imported from external source?
Is this code in transition between conventions?
Which convention should be used for this language?
Are there at least 3 instances of each convention to confirm mixing?
semantic_chunks: Inconsistent naming conventions increase cognitive load
Python should use snake_case; JavaScript/TypeScript should use camelCase
Mixed conventions in same file suggest incomplete refactoring or external imports
Fix: Choose appropriate convention and refactor all identifiers to match
agent_semantics.autofix_guidance: Cannot safely autofix without context - requires choosing correct convention and ensuring external APIs are not broken
